text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously optimistic - AI narrative is getting a second look (headline 5) and semiconductors/tech are holding up. The real driver for your names is whether the AI trade can sustain momentum into H2 without a catalyst reset.

Your holdings: SOXL (-4.76%) is the one to watch - it's a 3x leveraged semi ETF already underwater, and on margin that pain doubles fast; stop is $200, price is $242 so room exists, but any semi selloff hits this hardest. PTNM (-15.25%) is a tiny spec down hard with a stop at $9.50 vs $10.39 current - one bad tick away from a stop-out. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history; margin amplifies any gap-down severely.

Watch: (1) Whether SMH holds above ~$630 - that's your sector health gauge. (2) PTNM vs its $9.50 stop - it's close.

Bottom line: Your risk is concentrated in leveraged and speculative positions on margin - protect stops, don't add until SOXL and PTNM stabilize.
plays: PTNM is -15.25% and just $0.89 above your $9.50 stop - consider trimming or tightening the stop now; margin makes a gap-down through $9.50 very costly.
SOXL: scanner shows accumulate entry $233.75-$235.20, stop $230.22 - do NOT add here given you're already leveraged long on margin and underwater; respect your $200 stop instead.
No new buys today - existing risk (SOXL leverage, PTNM spec, SPCX IPO volatility, margin) is enough; let setups prove themselves before committing more capital.
